props.headline: Pages from JSON. Rendered instantly.
props.subheadline: Whilter.ai's Page Creation Engine turns a single JSON file into a fully validated, themed, production-ready web page — no backend, no CMS, no waiting.

props.heading: Three steps from JSON to live page
props.subheading: No deploy pipeline. No CMS login. No waiting for a developer. Just paste, validate, and render.

features.title: Paste Your JSON
features.description: Drop a JSON object into the Live Editor on the /demo route. Define page_metadata (title, slug, pageType) and a blocks array with type and props per block.

features.title: Engine Validates
features.description: Zod's discriminated union schema inspects every field in real time. If a required prop is missing or the wrong type, you see the exact error path before anything renders.

features.title: Instant UI Rendering
features.description: The Component Registry maps each block type to a lazy-loaded React component. The page renders live — animations, theme, and all — within milliseconds of your last keystroke.

props.heading: Built for speed. Designed for scale.

stats.description: Hero, Cards, Stats, FAQ, Table, Testimonials, CTA

props.heading: Whilter.ai vs. Traditional Approaches
props.caption: Capability comparison based on architectural requirements, not marketing claims.

faqs.question: What exactly is the Page Creation Engine?
faqs.answer: It is a purely frontend system built on Vite, React, and React Router. You write a JSON file that follows a strict Zod schema. The engine dynamically imports that file at runtime, validates it, and maps each block in the blocks array to a lazy-loaded React component from a registry. The result is a fully rendered, animated, themed web page with zero backend involvement.

faqs.question: How do I create a new page?
faqs.answer: Add a file at src/data/pages/your-slug.json following the schema. Navigate to /your-slug in the browser. Vite's import.meta.glob pre-registers all JSON files in that directory at build time, so the catch-all router picks it up automatically — no router changes, no redeploys.

faqs.question: How does the Theme Engine work?
faqs.answer: The ThemeContext injects CSS custom properties (--color-primary, --radius-card, --font-heading, etc.) directly onto document.documentElement whenever you switch themes. Every block component references these variables through inline styles. No props are threaded — adding a new block automatically inherits the active theme.

faqs.question: How do I add a new block type?
faqs.answer: Three steps: (1) Create the JSX component in src/components/blocks/. (2) Add its Zod object schema to the discriminatedUnion array in pageSchema.js. (3) Add one line to registry.js mapping the type string to a React.lazy() import. That block is now available in any page JSON.

faqs.question: Is this production-ready?
faqs.answer: Yes. npm run build produces a fully static dist/ folder. Each block component becomes its own async chunk via Rollup, so users only download what a given page needs. The import.meta.glob pattern gives Rollup a static manifest at build time — no runtime surprises. Deploy to any CDN (Vercel, Netlify, CloudFront) with zero configuration.

testimonials.quote: We replaced a six-month CMS migration with a JSON schema and a weekend sprint. The theme engine matched our brand guidelines exactly — without touching a single CSS file.
testimonials.author: Priya Mehta
testimonials.title: Head of Digital

testimonials.quote: The discriminated union validation was the key insight. Our content team can now edit JSON confidently — if the schema rejects a field, they see the exact path and message immediately.
testimonials.author: Daniel Osei
testimonials.title: Principal Engineer

testimonials.quote: Switching from Enterprise Sharp to Creative Soft in the demo took three seconds. That's when our CEO said 'ship it.' Pure client-side theming is a genuine superpower.
testimonials.author: Sarah Lindqvist
testimonials.title: VP Product

props.heading: See it live in 30 seconds.
props.subtext: Open the Live Demo, load a template, switch the theme, and watch pages render in real time. No signup. No backend. No waiting.
